channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Italian prime minister to meet with Trump on tariffs
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is expected to speak for Italy and the European Union as a whole.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Portrait of Palestinian boy who lost both arms is World Press Photo of the year for 2025
item.description: A portrait of a young Palestinian boy who lost both arms as a result of an Israeli attack in Gaza has been named World Press Photo of the year.
item.dc:creator: Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Power blackout hits all of Puerto Rico ahead of Easter weekend
item.description: All 1.4 million customers were affected. By late Wednesday night, crews had restored power to 175,000 customers, or 12%.
item.dc:creator: DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine's Kherson kill 1 person and wound 9
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian glide bombs and artillery struck a city in southern Ukraine on Wednesday, killing one person and wounding nine others as Moscow forces continued daily attacks across the country.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dog reunites with Israeli family after disappearing for 18 months in Gaza
item.description: BINYAMINA, Israel (AP) — Rachel Dancyg never thought she would see her dog again after it disappeared in the Hamas attack that sparked the ongoing war with Israel. Her ex-husband and brother were abducted from Kibbutz Nir Oz in southern Israel and killed.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Van Hollen denied meeting, phone call with Abrego Garcia during El Salvador visit
item.description: The Salvadoran government on Wednesday rebuffed Sen. Chris Van Hollen’s (D-Md.) request to meet or speak with a wrongfully deported Maryland man, the senator said, accusing the Trump administration and others of lying about Kilmar Abrego Garcia’s gang ties.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Man gets life in prison in killing of transgender model in the South Caucasus nation of Georgia
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — A man was convicted Wednesday of stabbing to death a transgender actor and model in her apartment in Georgia last year, a killing that shocked the South Caucasus nation and came a day after lawmakers passed legislation that severely restricted LGBTQ+ rights.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Maryland Sen. Van Hollen meets with El Salvador's vice president in push for Abrego Garcia's release
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen traveled to El Salvador on Wednesday and met with the country’s vice president to push for the release of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a man who was sent there by the Trump administration in March despite an immigration court order preventing his deportation.
item.dc:creator: Mary Clare Jalonick And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top Trump officials will hold talks with Europeans on the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Steve Witkoff, President Donald Trump’s special envoy, will travel to Paris this week for talks with European allies on U.S. efforts to negotiate a peace deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Senate Democrat asks government watchdog to audit Trump foreign assistance cuts
item.description: Sen. Jeff Merkley (D-Ore.), ranking member of the Senate Budget Committee and senior member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, is asking an independent government watchdog to analyze the consequences of President Trump’s foreign aid cuts.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Xi says China will stand with Southeast Asian countries in face of economic shocks
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping has told Malaysia’s leader that China will be a collaborative partner and stand with its Southeast Asian neighbors in the wake of global economic shocks.
item.dc:creator: Huizhong Wu, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia-linked hackers targeting European diplomats with invites to bogus wine tasting events
item.description: A Russia-linked hacking group is trying to get European diplomats to download malicious software through invitations to fake wine tasting events, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats fear chances of bringing home mistakenly deported man slipping away
item.description: Congressional Democrats are growing concerned that their hopes of bringing home a Maryland man who was wrongfully deported to a high-security prison in El Salvador may be slipping away, even as they struggle with how to push for his return.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, The Hill

item.title: Iran confirms that the 2nd round of nuclear talks with the US will be in Rome
item.description: Iranian state television has confirmed that the second round of Iran-U.S. nuclear talks will be held in Rome after earlier confusion over where the negotiations would take place.
item.dc:creator: Jon Gambrell And Nasser Karimi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American pastor kidnapped in South Africa rescued after deadly police shootout
item.description: American pastor Josh Sullivan, who was kidnapped from his church in the Eastern Cape Province last week, has been rescued by police following a "high-intensity shootout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO chief reaffirms support for Ukraine during a visit to the port city of Odesa
item.description: ODESA, Ukraine (AP) — NATO’s support for Ukraine remains “unwavering,” the alliance’s secretary-general said Tuesday, emphasizing that more than 20 billion euros — over $22 billion — in security assistance has already been pledged by NATO allies in the first three months of the year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Four journalists who were accused of working for Kremlin foe Navalny are convicted of extremism
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Tuesday convicted four journalists of extremism for working for an anti-corruption group founded by the late opposition leader Alexei Navalny and sentenced them to 5 1/2 years in prison each.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike outside a Gaza hospital kills a medic and wounds several
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — An Israeli airstrike hit the northern gate of the Kuwaiti Field Hospital in the Gaza Strip on Tuesday, killing a medic and wounding nine other people — all patients and medics — according to a spokesman for the hospital in the Muwasi area, where hundreds of thousands
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Russian general who criticized leadership may soon lead battalion of ex-cons: lawyer
item.description: Russia may soon reinstate a general who criticized Russia's top military leadership for blunders in Ukraine on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US pastor abducted at gunpoint while preaching in South Africa
item.description: Four men stole two cellphones from members of the congregation and kidnapped the pastor in his own truck.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, NewsNation

item.title: People detained in Myanmar after release from scam compounds attempt an escape
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — They walked out of the compound in Myanmar not knowing where they would go. Though they were aiming for the river that separated them from Thailand and freedom, they didn't know if they would make it across.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump floats deporting 'homegrown criminals' to El Salvador
item.description: Trump has suggested shipping off U.S. citizens who commit violent crimes to places like El Salvador's notorious mega-prison CECOT.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: US flies long-range bomber for drill with South Korea, as the North marks a key state anniversary
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The United States flew long-range B-1B bombers in a show of force against North Korea on Tuesday, days after the powerful sister of North Korean leader Kim Jong Un vowed to resist a U.S.-led push to eliminate the North’s nuclear program. North Korea often reacts to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN: Civilian death toll in Sudan's Darfur attacks tops 300 in 2 days as war nears 2-year mark
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — More than 300 civilians were killed in two days of intense fighting in Sudan’s conflict-wracked Darfur region, the U.N. humanitarian agency reported Monday, as the African country's brutal civil war nears the two-year mark.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia claims its deadly attack on Ukraine's Sumy targeted military forces as condemnation grows
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Russia on Monday claimed its deadly missile attack on Ukraine's Sumy that killed and wounded scores including children had targeted a gathering of Ukrainian troops, while European leaders condemned the attack as a war crime.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Putin sees Trump as 'an easy mark': John Bolton
item.description: During Monday's meeting with the press, Trump said of Ukraine: "When you start a war, you've gotta know that you can win the war.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Poland says Moscow is 'mocking' Trump with deadly Ukraine strike
item.description: Poland is urging the U.S. to respond to Russian President Vladimir Putin's "mocking" response to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure peace deal by striking civilians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza's southern city of Rafah and vows to 'vigorously' expand in the territory
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel announced Saturday it had completed construction of a new security corridor cutting off the southern city of Rafah from the rest of Gaza, and the military said it would soon expand “vigorously” in most of the small coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
